subject: Questions on map rescaling and low-pass filtering for template matching
body:
Dear all,

I am currently working on template matching and have a few questions
regarding the low-pass filtering and rescaling of maps.

I downloaded an EM density map from EMDB as a reference template (original
pixel size: 1.1538 Å/px). I low-pass filtered it to 60 Å using Relion Image
Handler, and then attempted to rescale it to 11.904 Å/px to match the pixel
size of my reconstructed tomograms.

During rescaling, Relion issued the following warning:

WARNING: Although the requested pixel size (--rescale_angpix) is 11.904
Å/px, the actual pixel size will be 12.2167 Å/px due to rounding of the box
size to an even number. The latter value is set in the image header. You
can overwrite the header pixel size by --force_header_angpix.

I then used --force_header_angpix to overwrite the header to 11.904 Å/px
and repeated the rescaling. This left me with a few questions:

(1) After forcing the header and rescaling again, is the actual pixel size
12.2167 Å/px or 11.904 Å/px? I understand Relion adjusts the box to even
numbers, but I am not sure if it can truly achieve 11.904 Å/px internally.
Has anyone dealt with this, or is there another package that allows precise
rescaling to 11.904 Å/px?

(2) The difference between 12.2167 Å/px and 11.904 Å/px is ~3%. Would this
difference significantly affect template matching in packages such as
Dynamo or GAPSTOP?

(3) In some packages (e.g., cryoCAT), a default Gaussian fall-off of 3
pixels is applied during low-pass filtering. I noticed Relion Image Handler
also has this option. Is such a Gaussian roll-off necessary for reference
templates? My impression is that it’s essential for generating soft-edge
masks, but I am not sure whether it’s also recommended for the reference
template.

Thank you in advance for your advice and experience!

Best regards,

Peng

-- 
Dr. Peng Liu

   Max-Planck-Institut für biophysikalische Chemie
   Am Faßberg 11, 37077 Göttingen
   Germany

    : +49(0)17635209768
   Email: peng.liu@mpibpc.mpg.de
